refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated algorithmic module that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies mission-context-driven computation rules, and outputs derived performance requirements with baseline metadata and audit-logged history.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Performance requirement derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, each computing requirements locally and synchronizing via a shared history store.
DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Template – Site Manager manually enters performance targets using a guided template; system records and versions the entries without algorithmic derivation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated algorithmic module that ingests worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints), applies mission-context-driven computation rules to derive cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, and safety margins, records the derived requirements as a timestamped baseline with full metadata, and maintains an audit-logged history of all baseline updates and calibration changes.

potential_sources: DP-A couples to worksite mission context input (must receive Tasking or equivalent mission data).
DP-A couples to baseline storage and history management (must interface with a persistent store or configuration management module).
DP-A couples to safety constraint validation (must receive or reference safety policies from Safety Services).
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is acceptable and necessary: mission context input is an external dependency (worksite), baseline storage is a logical dependency (traceability requirement), and safety constraint coupling is intentional (high safety criticality). No circular or hidden couplings detected. DP-A is decoupled from Vehicle and Teleoperation operational logic, reducing diffusion risk.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON, database record) at system initialization or on-demand.
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be validated against historical worksite data.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be incorrect or unsafe. Mitigation: require Site Manager validation of derived requirements before baseline lock.
RISK: Audit trail storage may grow large over time. Mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: Derivation algorithm may require frequent tuning as worksite conditions change. Mitigation: design algorithm as configurable rules engine, not hard-coded logic.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly state: (1) derivation algorithm shall be configurable (rules-based or ML-based) to accommodate different worksite types; (2) baseline lock mechanism shall require Site Manager approval before history entry; (3) audit trail shall include derivation method version and algorithm parameters used.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) as a core subsystem responsible for deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements from worksite mission context. The module integrates mission context inputs (from Site Manager via Tasking interface), safety constraints (from Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface), and provides performance baseline and history outputs (via Telemetry interface for reporting and via internal configuration store for operational use).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, operational policies) from Site Manager or mission planning system. Used as input to performance derivation algorithm.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints, policies, and safety-critical parameters from Safety Services. Used to derive safety margins and validate performance requirements against safety envelope.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance requirements, baseline metadata, and history records for reporting, audit, and traceability. Consumed by Site Manager, Safety Manager, and Maintenance/Quality for verification and compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for remote baseline updates, history synchronization, and audit log transmission in tele-operated or distributed scenarios.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary and the natural owner of cross-cutting performance requirement derivation. High containment: DP-A is a core system function, not a Vehicle-specific or Teleoperation-specific capability. Low diffusion: all interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are system-level, not scattered across Vehicle or Teleoperation. Ownership is clear: System Engineer or Project Manager.

Vehicle module is responsible for physical equipment operation and control. Performance derivation is a system-level planning function, not a vehicle-level operational function. If assigned to Vehicle, derivation logic would be duplicated or tightly coupled to vehicle-specific parameters, causing high diffusion. Not recommended.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and control. Performance derivation is a pre-operational planning function, not a real-time teleoperation function. If assigned to Teleoperation, derivation would be decoupled from mission planning and safety validation, causing high diffusion and poor traceability. Not recommended.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring, alerting, and constraint enforcement. Performance derivation includes safety margin calculation, but the broader function (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability derivation) is outside Safety Services scope. Partial containment only. If assigned to Safety Services, non-safety performance derivation would be orphaned. Not recommended.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (primary) with collaboration from Safety Manager (for safety margin validation) and Site Manager (for mission context validation). Project Manager provides oversight for traceability and audit compliance.
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is fully contained within NGHE Solution module. The module owns the derivation algorithm, baseline storage, and history management. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are system-level and well-defined. No diffusion to Vehicle or Teleoperation is required. Ownership is clear and unambiguous.
containment_risks: RISK: If baseline storage is implemented as a separate database or configuration management system outside NGHE Solution, ownership boundary becomes unclear. MITIGATION: Explicitly assign baseline storage to NGHE Solution or define a clear interface contract.

RISK: If Safety Services derives its own safety margins independently, audit trail and traceability may diverge. MITIGATION: Require Safety Services to consume safety margins from NGHE Solution derivation, not compute independently.

RISK: If Vehicle or Teleoperation modules need to adjust performance requirements in real-time (e.g., due to equipment degradation), DP-A may not support dynamic re-derivation. MITIGATION: Design DP-A to support on-demand re-derivation triggered by Vehicle or Teleoperation via a well-defined interface.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify that NGHE Solution module owns: (1) Performance Derivation Engine (algorithm and rules), (2) Baseline Storage (persistent record of derived requirements), (3) History Management (audit log and version control). Define explicit sub-interfaces: (a) Mission Context Input (from Tasking), (b) Safety Constraint Input (from SafetyAlert), (c) Baseline Output (to Telemetry and internal consumers), (d) History Query Interface (for audit and traceability reporting). Establish change control process: any update to baseline requires Site Manager approval and audit log entry.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) ingests worksite mission context (CR-NGHE-005.1), (2) computes performance requirements using mission-context-driven algorithms (CR-NGHE-005.2), (3) records derived requirements as baseline with metadata (CR-NGHE-005.3), (4) maintains managed history with audit trail (CR-NGHE-005.4), (5) provides traceability reporting (CR-NGHE-005.5). No gaps or misalignments. DP-A is a sound logical solution to the refined FR.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: DP-A is assigned to NGHE Solution, which is the system-level integration boundary. No diffusion to Vehicle or Teleoperation. Interfaces are system-level (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link), not scattered across modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score is 95/100. DP-A is a core system function, not a peripheral or optional capability. Ownership is clear: System Engineer. No competing claims from Vehicle or Teleoperation. Baseline storage and history management are logically part of NGHE Solution system architecture. Containment hypothesis is strong.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH: DP-A assigned to NGHE Solution enables clear verification: (1) test derivation algorithm against known mission contexts and validate output requirements; (2) verify baseline recording with metadata (timestamp, source, approver); (3) audit history log for completeness and correctness; (4) trace worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history for each performance target. All verification activities are feasible within NGHE Solution module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR: CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution module. Sub-FRs are allocated to specific functions within NGHE Solution: (1) Mission Context Ingestion (Tasking interface), (2) Derivation Algorithm (core engine), (3) Baseline Recording (storage subsystem), (4) History Management (audit subsystem), (5) Traceability Reporting (query/reporting subsystem). No ambiguity or overlap with other modules.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Explicitly define the Performance Derivation Engine as a sub-module within NGHE Solution with clear ownership (System Engineer) and interface contracts (Tasking in, SafetyAlert in, Telemetry out, Network Link inout).

REVISION 2: Establish a baseline lock/approval process: derived requirements must be reviewed and approved by Site Manager before being recorded as a baseline. This ensures traceability to worksite inputs and acceptance criteria compliance.
REVISION 3: Define retention and archival policy for history records to manage storage growth and compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.

REVISION 4: Specify algorithm versioning and parameter tracking: audit trail must record which derivation algorithm version and parameters were used for each baseline, enabling reproducibility and root-cause analysis.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 refined FR is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5). DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is a sound logical solution, and NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary with high containment (95/100) and low diffusion (5/100). All gates pass. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to define detailed design parameters and implementation modules for: (1) Mission Context Ingestion, (2) Derivation Algorithm, (3) Baseline Recording, (4) History Management, (5) Traceability Reporting. Proceeding to next decomposition level is necessary to achieve atomic, verifiable requirements and clear module ownership.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall ingest and validate worksite mission context (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count targets, operational policies, safety constraints) from Site Manager input via Tasking interface and store in a structured, machine-readable format.

CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms that map worksite parameters to equipment performance targets, with algorithm version and parameters recorded for audit.

CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements as a timestamped baseline with metadata (source, derivation method, algorithm version, approver) and require Site Manager approval before baseline lock.

CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why, algorithm version) and support history queries for traceability and compliance reporting.

CR-NGHE-005.5: The NGHE solution shall provide traceability reporting that links worksite mission inputs → derived performance requirements → baseline records → history updates, with evidence of approval and validation at each step.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON, database record) at system initialization or on-demand.
Performance derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be validated against historical worksite data.
Site Manager is available to approve derived baselines before lock.
Safety constraints from Safety Services are available and stable during derivation.
Audit trail storage and retention policies are defined by Maintenance/Quality and Project Manager.
open_risks: RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be incorrect or unsafe. MITIGATION: require Site Manager validation of derived requirements before baseline lock.
RISK: Audit trail storage may grow large over time. MITIGATION: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: Derivation algorithm may require frequent tuning as worksite conditions change. MITIGATION: design algorithm as configurable rules engine, not hard-coded logic.

RISK: If Safety Services derives its own safety margins independently, audit trail and traceability may diverge. MITIGATION: require Safety Services to consume safety margins from NGHE Solution derivation.

RISK: If Vehicle or Teleoperation modules need to adjust performance requirements in real-time, DP-A may not support dynamic re-derivation. MITIGATION: design DP-A to support on-demand re-derivation triggered via well-defined interface.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and sample data (from Site Manager).
Performance derivation algorithm specification and validation test cases (from System Engineer).
Baseline approval process and change control procedure (from Project Manager).
Safety constraint specification and safety margin calculation method (from Safety Manager).
Audit trail format and retention policy (from Maintenance/Quality).
Traceability reporting requirements and compliance criteria (from Project Manager and Safety Manager).
